Ghana

Panama

# Preview: Ghana vs Panama - prediction, team news, lineups

By Carter White| 15 Jun 2026 13:18, Last updated: 16 Jun 2026 15:29

© Imago / ZUMA Press Wire

Both looking to commence their World Cup campaigns on a positive note, Ghana and Panama clash at Toronto Stadium in the early hours of Thursday morning in Group L.

The Black Stars have struggled through a pile of friendly fixtures ahead of their opening match in Canada, whilst Los Canaleros have shown signs of considerable progression.

---

## Match preview

Not too long ago, Ghanaian football was rocked after their beloved national side failed to qualify for the Africa Cup of Nations for the first time in two decades, however, all will be forgotten if they can enjoy a productive summer in North America.

The Black Stars comfortably secured their spot at the World Cup with eight draws, one draw and just the single defeat in CAF qualifying, finishing as winners of Group I, six points ahead of second-placed Madagascar.

Since their concluding qualification match - a narrow success over Comoros in

news_items.title: Ghana vs Panama team news and predicted lineups | Squawka | EN
news_items.text:
# Ghana vs Panama team news and predicted lineups | Squawka | EN

Published: 2026-06-15T18:00:00+00:00
Source: squawka.com (squawka.com)
Language: en

## Story

Ghana vs Panama team news and predicted lineups | Squawka | EN

Squawka/ News/ World Cup/ Ghana vs Panama team news and predicted lineups

# Ghana vs Panama team news and predicted lineups

- Dean Smith
- 13 minutes ago

- Copied

Ghana begin their 2026 World Cup campaign with what they will consider a must-win fixture against Panama in Toronto on Thursday June 18 (00:00 UK time).

It’s been a strange preparation for the tournament for the Black Stars, with Carlos Queiroz only replacing former boss Otto Addo just two months ago. A failed Afcon qualifying campaign proved to spell the end for Addo in his second spell in charge.

Queiroz becomes the second manager to coach at five consecutive World Cups as he leads his team out against Panama. The Portuguese boss knows that anything less than three points will leave them with a huge mountain to climb.

news_items.title: Ghana vs. Panama - kick-off time, team news, how to watch Black Stars' FIFA World Cup opener - ESPN
news_items.author: Leonard Solms
news_items.text:
Ghana vs. Panama - kick-off time, team news, how to watch Black Stars' FIFA World Cup opener - ESPN

Skip to main content Skip to navigation

play

Ghana will kick off their FIFA World Cup campaign on Wednesday in Toronto, with a Group L clash against Panama.

The Black Stars missed the 2025 Africa Cup of Nations(AFCON) in Morocco, but this tournament offers them some hope of redemption under Carlos Queiroz.

Queiroz replaced Otto Addo in April, with the latter having been controversially dismissed despite guiding Ghana to World Cup qualification.

Ironically, Queiroz himself is widely understood to have been forced out by one of Ghana's rivals - South Africa- after guiding them to the 2002 World Cup in Japan and South Korea.

There, Bafana Bafana were led by Jomo Sono after Queiroz resigned following the appointment of Sono as a technical director - an appointment which the Portuguese coach felt was designed to undermine him.

Queiroz took charge of Iran right before the 2022 World Cup, giving him experience of steadying the ship in a limited timeframe. However, his previous eight-year stint with them gave him at least some foresight, which he has had to do without in the Ghana ho

news_items.title: Ghana predicted XI vs. Panama: Possible lineup for World Cup clash in Toronto - Sports Mole
news_items.text:
Ghana predicted XI vs. Panama: Possible lineup for World Cup clash in Toronto - Sports Mole

Menu

World Cup Gameweek 1

Toronto Stadium

# Ghana predicted XI vs. Panama: Possible lineup for World Cup clash in Toronto

By Carter White| 15 Jun 2026 13:29, Last updated: 15 Jun 2026 13:40

© Imago

Ghana are set to be without the experience of Thomas Partey for their World Cup opener against Panama on Thursday morning.

The former Arsenal man will miss the match at Toronto Stadium after being denied entry into the country by the Canadian government.

As a result, the Black Stars need to find a new midfield partner for talented youngster Caleb Marfo Yirenkyi, who has been likened to Ghanaian hero Michael Essien.

Playing 29 matches for Auxerre in Ligue 1 this season, Elisha Owusu is the likely candidate to fill the gap in the middle of the park.

After netting seven goals during World Cup qualifying, Jordan Ayew has vowed to make his country proud on the biggest stage.

Ghana's wide options are the highlight of their squad, with Manchester City star Antoine Semenyo preparing for his first World Cup.

On the other flank, Leicester City's Abdul Fatawu could be kept out of the XI by the e

news_items.title: Panama predicted XI vs. Ghana: Possible lineup for World Cup opener in Toronto - Sports Mole
news_items.text:
Panama predicted XI vs. Ghana: Possible lineup for World Cup opener in Toronto - Sports Mole

Menu

World Cup Gameweek 1

Toronto Stadium

# Panama predicted XI vs. Ghana: Possible lineup for World Cup opener in Toronto

By Carter White| 15 Jun 2026 13:40

© Imago

Panama are expected to be without star player Adalberto Carrasquilla for large portions of their World Cup opener against Ghana on Thursday morning.

The Pumas midfielder has not played any of his side's warm-up fixtures due to a muscular injury sustained whilst on club duty.

However, the 27-year-old was pictured in training on Sunday and could feature off the bench at Toronto Stadium versus the Black Stars.

Capable of playing at centre-back and in the engine room, Carlos Harvey could be Carrasquilla's replacement in midfield on matchday one.

A consistent goalscorer for Los Canaleros over the past four years, Jose Fajardo should start in attack at Toronto Stadium.

Occupying a position further back in the XI, Anibal Godoy is the second-oldest player in the Panama squad at 36.

Scoring in Los Canaleros' most recent friendly victory over the Dominican Republic on June 4, Tomas Rodriguez should start on one of the flanks

# Preview: Ghana vs Panama - prediction, team news, lineups

By Carter White| 15 Jun 2026 13:18, Last updated: 16 Jun 2026 15:29

© Imago / ZUMA Press Wire

Both looking to commence their World Cup campaigns on a positive note, Ghana and Panama clash at Toronto Stadium in the early hours of Thursday morning in Group L.

The Black Stars have struggled through a pile of friendly fixtures ahead of their opening match in Canada, whilst Los Canaleros have shown signs of considerable progression.

---

## Match preview

Not too long ago, Ghanaian football was rocked after their beloved national side failed to qualify for the Africa Cup of Nations for the first time in two decades, however, all will be forgotten if they can enjoy a productive summer in North America.

The Black Stars comfortably secured their spot at the World Cup with eight draws, one draw and just the single defeat in CAF qualifying, finishing as winners of Group I, six points ahead of second-placed Madagascar.

Since their concluding qualification match - a narrow success over Comoros in "}

# Ghana vs Panama team news and predicted lineups

- Dean Smith
- 13 minutes ago

- Copied

Ghana begin their 2026 World Cup campaign with what they will consider a must-win fixture against Panama in Toronto on Thursday June 18 (00:00 UK time).

It’s been a strange preparation for the tournament for the Black Stars, with Carlos Queiroz only replacing former boss Otto Addo just two months ago. A failed Afcon qualifying campaign proved to spell the end for Addo in his second spell in charge.

Queiroz becomes the second manager to coach at five consecutive World Cups as he leads his team out against Panama. The Portuguese boss knows that anything less than three points will leave them with a huge mountain to climb.

Ghana will kick off their FIFA World Cup campaign on Wednesday in Toronto, with a Group L clash against Panama.

The Black Stars missed the 2025 Africa Cup of Nations(AFCON) in Morocco, but this tournament offers them some hope of redemption under Carlos Queiroz.

Queiroz replaced Otto Addo in April, with the latter having been controversially dismissed despite guiding Ghana to World Cup qualification.

Ironically, Queiroz himself is widely understood to have been forced out by one of Ghana's rivals - South Africa- after guiding them to the 2002 World Cup in Japan and South Korea.

There, Bafana Bafana were led by Jomo Sono after Queiroz resigned following the appointment of Sono as a technical director - an appointment which the Portuguese coach felt was designed to undermine him.

Queiroz took charge of Iran right before the 2022 World Cup, giving him experience of steadying the ship in a limited timeframe. However, his previous eight-year stint with them gave him at least some foresight, which he has had to do without in the Ghana ho"}

# Ghana predicted XI vs. Panama: Possible lineup for World Cup clash in Toronto

By Carter White| 15 Jun 2026 13:29, Last updated: 15 Jun 2026 13:40

© Imago

Ghana are set to be without the experience of Thomas Partey for their World Cup opener against Panama on Thursday morning.

The former Arsenal man will miss the match at Toronto Stadium after being denied entry into the country by the Canadian government.

As a result, the Black Stars need to find a new midfield partner for talented youngster Caleb Marfo Yirenkyi, who has been likened to Ghanaian hero Michael Essien.

Playing 29 matches for Auxerre in Ligue 1 this season, Elisha Owusu is the likely candidate to fill the gap in the middle of the park.

After netting seven goals during World Cup qualifying, Jordan Ayew has vowed to make his country proud on the biggest stage.

Ghana's wide options are the highlight of their squad, with Manchester City star Antoine Semenyo preparing for his first World Cup.

On the other flank, Leicester City's Abdul Fatawu could be kept out of the XI by the e"}
{"title": "Panama predicted XI vs. Ghana: Possible lineup for World Cup opener in Toronto - Sports Mole", "url": "https://www.sportsmole.co.uk/football/ghana/world-cup-2026/predicted-lineups/carrasquilla-out-panama-predicted-xi-vs-ghana_599287.html", "publishedDate": "2026-06-15T12:40:04.000Z", "author": null, "text": "Panama predicted XI vs. Ghana: Possible lineup for World Cup opener in Toronto - Sports Mole

World Cup Gameweek 1

# Panama predicted XI vs. Ghana: Possible lineup for World Cup opener in Toronto

By Carter White| 15 Jun 2026 13:40

© Imago

Panama are expected to be without star player Adalberto Carrasquilla for large portions of their World Cup opener against Ghana on Thursday morning.

The Pumas midfielder has not played any of his side's warm-up fixtures due to a muscular injury sustained whilst on club duty.

However, the 27-year-old was pictured in training on Sunday and could feature off the bench at Toronto Stadium versus the Black Stars.

Capable of playing at centre-back and in the engine room, Carlos Harvey could be Carrasquilla's replacement in midfield on matchday one.

A consistent goalscorer for Los Canaleros over the past four years, Jose Fajardo should start in attack at Toronto Stadium.

Occupying a position further back in the XI, Anibal Godoy is the second-oldest player in the Panama squad at 36.

Scoring in Los Canaleros' most recent friendly victory over the Dominican Republic on June 4, Tomas Rodriguez should start on one of the flanks"}
